text: Delivered private[gale] DenseSpectralKernels (core/shared, both platforms): tridiagonalize (EISPACK tred2, scale-normalized Householder, values-only path skips Q), symmetricTridiagonalEigen (tql2, Wilkinson shift, ascending + lockstep vector permutation — the Lanczos projected-problem entry point), symmetricEigen (composed), hessenberg (orthes/ortran), nonsymmetricEigen (hqr2 Francis double-shift + real-Schur back-substitution, exact conjugate-pair convention accepted directly by NonsymmetricEigenDecomposition, unit-norm packed vectors). Iterative solvers return Either[SpectralKernelFailure, _] (tql2: 30 sweeps/eigenvalue; Francis: 30n total). Deterministic cross-platform: hand-rolled overflow-safe pythag, scala.math only, no hypot/FMA. Implemented by subagent; adversarial review subagent DIED on account spend limit, so the line-by-line EISPACK-fidelity review was done inline by the lead instead: traced tred2 rank-2 update + in-place Q accumulation, tql2 recurrences/underflow restart, hqr2 shift/deflation/bulge-chase/back-substitution branches against reference — port faithful, no bugs found; NaN input provably terminates via sweep budgets; values-only provably identical to full solve. One test assertion precedence typo fixed by lead (unit-norm check in n=1 case). 28 new tests incl. analytic Laplacian spectrum, repeated eigenvalues, companion/rotation fixed spectra, trace/det identities, residuals through the packed decode path, exact pairing on random matrices, zero-budget failure Lefts. Verified: coreJVM 196/196, coreJS 196/196, laws 17/17 both.
